candidate_summary: Lightweight rapid-iteration attacker-defender LLM coevolution inspired by Tuareg takouba sword-shield combat, emphasizing many small cycles over few large ones, with budget mobility constraint B_A + B_D <= B_total and Red-Queen archive validation where each new defender is re-tested on full historical attack archive.

results.title: Efficient Adversarial Training Continuous Attacks (2405.15589)
results.rationale: Focuses on continuous-embedding attacks for faster adversarial training, not iterative attacker-defender coevolution with archive validation. No domain overlap with Tuareg/sword-shield framing.

results.title: Covolve Two-Player Zero-Sum Coevolution (OpenReview)
results.rationale: Two-player zero-sum LLM coevolution shares coevolution structure but lacks the lightweight many-cycle, budget-mobility constraint, and historical archive Red-Queen validation specific to the candidate.

results.title: Multi-Agent Evolve LLM Self-Improve (2510.23595)
results.rationale: Proposer+Solver+Judge closed loop is self-improvement, not adversarial attacker-defender with budget split and historical archive.

results.title: MIXAT Continuous+Discrete Adversarial (2505.16947)
results.rationale: Mixes continuous and discrete adversarial training; doesn't address iterative coevolution cycles, budget mobility, or archive-based defender validation.

results.title: Digital Red Queen Sakana LLM Coevolution
results.rationale: Direct match: Red Queen Hypothesis coevolution where each new warrior must beat predecessors is functionally equivalent to the Red-Queen historical-attack-archive validation. Strong keyword overlap, high semantic similarity, and high functional equivalence.

results.title: Adversarial Tuning Defending Jailbreak (2406.06622)
results.rationale: Two-stage adversarial tuning with meta-universal prompts; lacks lightweight many-cycle coevolution and budget constraint formulation.

results.title: Adaptive Jailbreak Defense Self-Evolving (OpenReview)
results.rationale: Self-evolving adaptive defense learning from attack history overlaps with historical-attack-archive validation concept. Keyword count meets threshold (>=2).

results.title: Jailbreak Attacks Defenses Survey (2407.04295)
results.rationale: General survey, not a specific coevolutionary mechanism comparable to the candidate.

decision_rationale: Candidate FAILS novelty verification. Result 5 (Digital Red Queen Sakana LLM Coevolution) is a strong match on all three axes (kw=4, sem=0.82, func=0.78), directly capturing the Red-Queen historical-attack-archive validation mechanism where each new defender/warrior must beat all predecessors. Result 7 (Adaptive Jailbreak Defense Self-Evolving) also hits on keyword overlap (>=2) with attack-history-based self-evolving defense. The Tuareg/takouba domain framing is novel surface dressing, but the core mechanism (lightweight iterative attacker-defender coevolution with Red-Queen archive validation) is substantially anticipated by prior art.
